title: Cisco ACI Contract / Filter Hit Analysis, Permit/Deny Counter Trending, and Policy-Enforcement Visibility
exclusions: Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS standalone-mode switches (use UC-18.4.* in the Cisco Nexus Dashboard subcategory; standalone uses VXLAN-EVPN policy not vzBrCP); VMware NSX distributed firewall (use UC-18.2.* in the VMware NSX subcategory; DFW has its own rule taxonomy); Cisco Secure Firewall (Firepower / FTD / ASA) policy hits — that data flows through the Cisco eStreamer use cases under cat-04; Cisco Hyperflex application-aware firewalling (use cat-19); APIC clusters running 4.x or earlier (out of support since Mar 2024).
dataSources: `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) combining **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:contract_stats`** JSON emitted by collectors polling **`actrlPermitStats5min`**, **`actrlDropStats5min`**, **`actrlPermitStats15min`** optional grains, **`actrlEntity`** statistics; **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:contract_config`** capturing **`vzBrCP`**, **`vzSubj`**, **`vzFilter`**, **`vzEntry`**, **`fvRsCons`**, **`fvRsProv`**, **`fvAEPg`** MO snapshots via **`GET https://<apic>/api/class/vzBrCP.json?query-target=children`** and **`GET https://<apic>/api/node/class/fvAEPg.json?rsp-subtree-include=fvRsCons,fvRsProv`**; **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** from **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** for APIC leaf/spine narratives plus **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`**/**`cisco:aci:audit`** when operators poll **`faultInst`** or **`aaaModLR`** outside syslog streams.
app: **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (Splunkbase **4022**, package directory **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci**, versions near **2.0.x**) for syslog normalization into **cisco:aci:syslog**; **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, Splunkbase **1546**) or Python scripted collectors emitting **cisco:aci:contract_stats** / **cisco:aci:contract_config**; optional **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** (Splunkbase **7777**) when dashboards span broader Cisco portfolios.
spl:
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:contract_stats" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:contract_config" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault")
earliest=-24h@m
| eval mo_class=coalesce(classId, mo_class, object_type, "")
| eval dn_lc=lower(coalesce(dn, distinguished_name, ""))
| eval rule_action=upper(trim(coalesce(actionT, action_t, ruleAction, "")))
| eval permit_pkt=tonumber(coalesce(permitPktCum, permitPkts, permit_pkt_cum, pktCum))
| eval permit_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(permitBytesCum, permit_bytes, bytesCum))
| eval drop_pkt=tonumber(coalesce(dropPktCum, dropPkts, deny_pkt_cum, denyPkts))
| eval prio_num=tonumber(coalesce(prio, priority, rulePrio))
| eval contract_hint=case(
    mo_class="vzBrCP", "vzBrCP_bridge_domain_contract",
    mo_class="vzSubj", "vzSubj_subject",
    mo_class="vzFilter", "vzFilter_l4_filter",
    mo_class="vzEntry", "vzEntry_five_tuple",
    mo_class="fvRsCons", "fvRsCons_consumed_side",
    mo_class="fvRsProv", "fvRsProv_provided_side",
    mo_class="actrlRule", "actrlRule_tcam_program",
    mo_class="actrlPermitStats5min", "actrlPermitStats5min_counters",
    mo_class="actrlDropStats5min", "actrlDropStats5min_counters",
    mo_class="fvAEPg", "fvAEPg_application_epg",
    1=1, "other_mo")
| stats sum(permit_pkt) as permit_sum,
        sum(drop_pkt) as drop_sum,
        sum(permit_bytes) as permit_bytes_sum,
        latest(rule_action) as action_latest,
        latest(prio_num) as prio_latest,
        values(contract_hint) as mo_roles,
        dc(mo_class) as mo_depth
    by dn_lc
| eval total_pkt=permit_sum+drop_sum
| eval deny_ratio=if(total_pkt>0, round(100*drop_sum/total_pkt,3), null())
| eval scope_tag=case(
    match(dn_lc,"uni/tn-[^/]+/ctx-"), "context_scoped_contract",
    match(dn_lc,"/brcdef"), "vzAny_default_contract",
    match(dn_lc,"/brc-"), "named_vzBrCP",
    match(dn_lc,"actrlrule"), "leaf_actrlcompiled",
    1=1, "scope_review_in_apic")
| where match(dn_lc,"(vzbrcp|vzsubj|vzfilter|vzentry|fvrscons|fvrsprov|actrlrule|actrlpermitstats5min|actrldropstats5min|fvae)") OR drop_sum>0 OR permit_sum>0
| sort - drop_sum
| head 250
| table dn_lc permit_sum drop_sum deny_ratio prio_latest action_latest scope_tag mo_roles mo_depth
description: Monitors Cisco Application Centric Infrastructure policy enforcement through tenant **vzBrCP** contracts with **vzSubj** subjects, **vzFilter**/**vzEntry** Layer-4 tuples, compiled **actrlRule** rows (**actionT**, **prio**, **scopeId**) paired with rolling **actrlPermitStats5min**/**actrlDropStats5min** counters, **fvAEPg** associations exposed via **fvRsCons**/**fvRsProv**, **vzAny** default shortcuts, contract **scope** boundaries (**context**, **tenant**, **application-profile**, **global**), **Preferred Group** memberships, **fvESg** Endpoint Security Group overlays, and syslog faults (**F4029**, **F4030**) so Splunk reveals permit/deny drift, silent TCAM exhaustion, orphan bindings, and stretched-fabric synchronization gaps ahead of invisible lateral movement.
value: East-west posture hides inside compiled TCAM—not merely dashboards listing tenants—because permit spikes may trace **vzAny** misuse while deny bursts might signal deliberate microsegmentation or a partial enforcement fault after TCAM saturation. Bringing REST-fed counters, MO relationships, syslog faults, and orchestrator-aware timelines together collapses noisy bridges across NetOps and security architects whenever firewall migrations or stretched-site edits temporarily distort counters.
implementation: Deploy **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** syslog plus scripted HTTPS collectors authenticated via **aaaLogin.json**/**aaaRefresh.json** polling **vzBrCP**, **actrlRule**, **actrlPermitStats5min**, **actrlDropStats5min**, **fvAEPg**, **fvRsCons**, **fvRsProv** MO classes into **cisco:aci:contract_stats**/**contract_config** sourcetypes; normalize FIELDALIAS macros for APIC attributes; schedule Splunk alerts comparing deny velocities against baseline permits while correlating **faultInst** codes referencing TCAM programming.
visualization: (1) Timechart **drop_sum** versus **permit_sum** split by **scope_tag**. (2) Heatmap **deny_ratio** × **contract_hint**. (3) Table worst **dn_lc** tuples with **prio_latest**, **rule_action**, **mo_roles**. (4) Single-value tracker for **F4029**/**F4030** syslog counts sourced from **cisco:aci:syslog**. (5) Sankey-style chord (lookup-assisted) linking **fvRsCons**/**fvRsProv** toward **vzBrCP** contracts.
schema: CIM: Network_Traffic permit/deny telemetry plus Inventory overlays for vzBrCP relationships
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Network_Traffic once FIELDALIAS maps permit/drop counters into bytes/packets semantics; accelerate Inventory for stable vzBrCP dn anchors referenced across polls.

references.title: Cisco APIC Basic Configuration Guide (contracts chapter), Release 6.0(x)
Cisco ACI Management Information Model Reference
Cisco ACI Contract Strategy White Paper

knownFalsePositives: **Firewall-consolidation cutovers** — coordinated deny-counter surges appear while engineers rewrite **vzBrCP** subjects during staged migrations—traffic remains legitimately blocked until replacement permits publish; correlate Splunk timelines with CRQ identifiers referencing legacy firewall retirement milestones rather than paging lateral-movement hunts alone. **Preferred Group membership toggles** — flipping **prefGrMemb** from excluded toward included briefly yields permit-counter troughs until TCAM reprograms relaxed intra-group shortcuts—suppress alerts aligned with tenant escape-hatch approvals documented in CAB packets. **Scheduled interconnect-maintenance windows between pods** — IP-network instability delays collectors fetching **actrlPermitStats5min** snapshots while dataplane forwarding stays healthy—cross-reference **_time** jitter against WAN maintenance bridges before accusing microsegmentation regressions. **Leaf TCAM exhaustion or partial programming** — **F4029**/**F4030** combinations drop counters silently because rules never compiled yet syslog fault queues lag REST pulls—pair deny dashboards with fault streams before trusting zero-hit narratives. **vzAny default shortcuts** — east-west conversations traverse broad **/brcdef** permits making per-EPG counters look idle despite alive flows—confirm with SPAN captures rather than concluding Splunk misses ingestion. **NDO/MSO stretched-site synchronization drift** — templates propagate slower than primary-site edits—secondary-site deny spikes occasionally reflect orchestration backlog rather than hostile bursts until replicated **vzBrCP** scopes converge.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• APIC clusters on supported 6.0(x) / 6.1(x) releases with HTTPS 443/tcp reachable from Splunk collectors; dedicate read-only automation credentials separate from interactive administrators.
• Install Splunk_TA_cisco-aci (Splunkbase 4022, 2.0.x field releases) wherever APIC and fabric syslog terminates so **cisco:aci:syslog** ingests mnemonic faults pertinent to contracts and TCAM (**F4029** contract not deployed, **F4030** partial programming success, **F1742** orphan contract references, **F0467** VLAN conflicts, **F2102** border-leaf VRF leakage) as peer evidence to REST statistics.
• Layer rest_ta (Splunkbase 1546) or Python modular inputs that POST aaaLogin.json, refresh via aaaRefresh.json (~10 minute cookie/token TTL), then GET **/api/class/vzBrCP.json?query-target=children**, **/api/class/actrlRule.json**, **/api/class/actrlPermitStats5min.json**, **/api/class/actrlDropStats5min.json**, **/api/class/actrlEntity.json?rsp-subtree-include=stats**, **/api/node/class/fvAEPg.json?rsp-subtree-include=fvRsCons,fvRsProv**—tag streams cisco:aci:contract_stats for counters and cisco:aci:contract_config for **vzBrCP**, **vzSubj**, **vzFilter**, **vzEntry**, plus optional cisco:aci:fault / cisco:aci:audit sourcetypes mirroring faultInst and aaaModLR edits.
• RBAC remains sensitive because read-only automation accounts still traverse broad tenants—segment collectors when auditors demand separation between statistics pulls versus configuration reconciliation jobs hitting vzBrCP trees.
• Capacity planning: polling **actrlPermitStats5min** across every leaf explodes cardinality—budget indexer EPS when joins multiply **dn_lc** × **mo_class** facets ahead of alerting.

Step 1 — Configure ingestion mapped to MO semantics
(1) Syslog lane: validate **cisco:aci:syslog** surfaces TCAM/programming faults tied to contracts—not merely optics noise—before trusting deny ratios derived solely from counters.
(2) Stats lane: schedule five-minute pulls for **actrlPermitStats5min** and **actrlDropStats5min**, optionally layering **actrlPermitStats15min**, **actrlPermitStats1h**, **actrlPermitStats1d**, plus paired drop grains—flatten APIC attributes such as pktCum, bytesCum, parent **dn**, **prio** (1–15), **scopeId**, **actionT** (**permit**, **deny**, **log**, **redirect**).
(3) Contract lane: enumerate **vzBrCP** objects with children exposing **vzSubj**, **vzFilter**, **vzEntry** tuples (EtherType IP, TCP/UDP/ICMP protocols, source/destination port ranges, optional **tcpFlags**) while capturing **scope** (**context**, **tenant**, **application-profile**, **global**) and contract **prio** (**level1**–**level3**) plus **reverseFilterPorts** for asymmetric subjects.
(4) Binding lane: ingest **fvAEPg** subtrees listing **fvRsCons** versus **fvRsProv** attachments toward provider/consumed **vzBrCP** contracts so dashboards reconcile east-west expectations without guessing from counters alone.
(5) Deny lane: optionally harvest **GET /api/class/actrlRule.json?query-target-filter=eq(actrlRule.actionT,"deny")** nightly to spotlight deny-heavy TCAM rows correlated with **fvESg** IP microsegmentation when present alongside legacy **fvAEPg** constructs.

Step 2 — Build Splunk correlation for permits, denies, and bindings
Paste into a saved search once indexes align; extend coalesce() chains after running fieldsummary against raw JSON.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:contract_stats" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:contract_config" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault")
earliest=-24h@m
| eval mo_class=coalesce(classId, mo_class, object_type, "")
| eval dn_lc=lower(coalesce(dn, distinguished_name, ""))
| eval rule_action=upper(trim(coalesce(actionT, action_t, ruleAction, "")))
| eval permit_pkt=tonumber(coalesce(permitPktCum, permitPkts, permit_pkt_cum, pktCum))
| eval permit_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(permitBytesCum, permit_bytes, bytesCum))
| eval drop_pkt=tonumber(coalesce(dropPktCum, dropPkts, deny_pkt_cum, denyPkts))
| eval prio_num=tonumber(coalesce(prio, priority, rulePrio))
| eval contract_hint=case(
    mo_class="vzBrCP", "vzBrCP_bridge_domain_contract",
    mo_class="vzSubj", "vzSubj_subject",
    mo_class="vzFilter", "vzFilter_l4_filter",
    mo_class="vzEntry", "vzEntry_five_tuple",
    mo_class="fvRsCons", "fvRsCons_consumed_side",
    mo_class="fvRsProv", "fvRsProv_provided_side",
    mo_class="actrlRule", "actrlRule_tcam_program",
    mo_class="actrlPermitStats5min", "actrlPermitStats5min_counters",
    mo_class="actrlDropStats5min", "actrlDropStats5min_counters",
    mo_class="fvAEPg", "fvAEPg_application_epg",
    1=1, "other_mo")
| stats sum(permit_pkt) as permit_sum,
        sum(drop_pkt) as drop_sum,
        sum(permit_bytes) as permit_bytes_sum,
        latest(rule_action) as action_latest,
        latest(prio_num) as prio_latest,
        values(contract_hint) as mo_roles,
        dc(mo_class) as mo_depth
    by dn_lc
| eval total_pkt=permit_sum+drop_sum
| eval deny_ratio=if(total_pkt>0, round(100*drop_sum/total_pkt,3), null())
| eval scope_tag=case(
    match(dn_lc,"uni/tn-[^/]+/ctx-"), "context_scoped_contract",
    match(dn_lc,"/brcdef"), "vzAny_default_contract",
    match(dn_lc,"/brc-"), "named_vzBrCP",
    match(dn_lc,"actrlrule"), "leaf_actrlcompiled",
    1=1, "scope_review_in_apic")
| where match(dn_lc,"(vzbrcp|vzsubj|vzfilter|vzentry|fvrscons|fvrsprov|actrlrule|actrlpermitstats5min|actrldropstats5min|fvae)") OR drop_sum>0 OR permit_sum>0
| sort - drop_sum
| head 250
| table dn_lc permit_sum drop_sum deny_ratio prio_latest action_latest scope_tag mo_roles mo_depth
```

Correlation notes (what each stanza emphasizes)
• mo_class keeps **vzBrCP** configuration distinct from compiled **actrlRule** rows versus rolling **actrlPermitStats5min** / **actrlDropStats5min** measurements—critical because counters omit GUI-friendly labels unless joined later.
• contract_hint surfaces **fvRsCons**/**fvRsProv** attachments separately from **vzEntry** tuples so analysts detect **vzAny** shortcuts (**/brcdef**) inflating permits without symmetrical consumed/provider matrices.
• deny_ratio amplifies asymmetric bursts—always compare against **rule_action** before blaming microsegmentation drift alone.
• scope_tag separates intra-VRF **ctx** paths from **vzAny** markers versus leaf-local **actrlRule** namespaces—validate inside APIC MITREE viewers whenever Splunk hints feel ambiguous.
• prio_latest echoes Cisco contract priority (**level1** highest)—investigate when lower tiers appear dominant because **vzAny** inserts wide permits or **Preferred Group** memberships bypass exhaustive contract hops.

Step 3 — Validate against APIC operators consoles
(1) Compare Splunk **dn_lc** samples against APIC Managed Object browsers—numeric counter deltas wider than twice the poll cadence imply duplicated ingestion or stale aaaRefresh.json rotation.
(2) Verify **actrlPermitStats5min** aggregates versus APIC Statistics views tied to matching **actrlRule** distinguished names— Splunk discrepancies frequently trace camelCase mismatches remediated via FIELDALIAS macros.
(3) Validate **fvRsCons**/**fvRsProv** symmetry inside Tenants → Application Profiles → EPGs → Contracts panes—misaligned direction appears as deny spikes despite steady permit counters elsewhere.
(4) When **scope_tag** labels rows **vzAny_default_contract**, expect sparse per-EPG counters because east-west shortcuts bypass granular **vzEntry** hits—confirm behavior with SPAN/capture policies rather than concluding Splunk ingestion gaps prematurely.
(5) Cross-site fabrics: contrast Splunk timelines with Nexus Dashboard Orchestrator (NDO/MSO) template revisions—laggy stretched deployments skew deny ratios until replicated **vzBrCP** scopes converge.

Step 4 — Operationalise workspaces and suppression macros
(1) Dashboard stack: timechart drop totals by **scope_tag**, heatmaps for **deny_ratio**, drill tables tying **fvRsCons**/**fvRsProv** DNs to CMDB owners.
(2) Alerts on sustained drop_sum velocity require ServiceNow correlation with **vzBrCP** edits—suppress when aaaModLR shows approved change bridges.
(3) Publish TCAM pressure tiles summarizing F4029/F4030 fault rates per leaf so teams connect silent counter misses with hardware rule budgets (for example Nexus 9300-FX vs 9300-GX2 ceilings).
(4) Escalate Preferred Group escape hatches (prefGrMemb=include) through tenant governance chats when Splunk audits show inclusion toggles coinciding with permit surges—pair with microsegmentation OKRs.
(5) Rotate automation secrets quarterly—failed aaaRefresh attempts often surface as duplicated events with identical counters but drifting _indextime offsets.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting modes and misleading readings

• **Duplicate dn_lc duplicates** — multiple modular inputs authenticated to the same APIC VIP double counts permit sums until you enrich with collector_id metadata.

• **Null permit_pkt columns** — field aliases missing vendor-specific camelCase (pktCum vs **pktPerBurst**) — inspect `_raw` JSON and broaden coalesce() chains cautiously after vendor confirmation.

• **Syslog denies without dropping counters** — hardware queue narratives may precede REST aggregation—tie syslog timestamps to REST **_time** windows before paging microsegmentation SMEs.

• **Scope churn spikes** — migrating **vzBrCP** **scope** between **context** and **tenant** forces TCAM rebuilds—expect transient deny jitter unrelated to lateral movement alerts.

• **Empty fvRsCons despite flows** — traffic rides **vzAny**—join configuration sourcetypes referencing **/brcdef** contracts before concluding Splunk ingestion gaps.

• **NDO/MSO synchronization drift** — secondary-site counters lag primary edits—delay paging until orchestrator dashboards confirm template convergence across sites.

• **fvESg overlaps** — IP-based Endpoint Security Groups may steer drops onto alternate distinguished names—extend MO polling lists beyond legacy **fvAEPg** paths.

• **prio_latest textual mismatch** — APIC GUI lists **level1** tokens while REST emits integers—normalize inside eval before sorting priorities.

• **HTTP 429 storms** — widen intervals or shard MO pulls whenever APIC CPU saturates during massive **vzFilter** consolidation batches.

• **Silent misses post-TCAM exhaustion** — correlate **F4029** faults with abrupt drops in observed counters even before deny ratios spike—TCAM misses imply enforcement gaps rather than benign drift.

• **Audit-only aaaModLR trails** — capture contract edits yet omit dataplane verification—never rely solely on audits without concurrent **actrlPermitStats5min** deltas.

• **Collector TLS failures masked as sparse data** — splunkd logs often reveal handshake errors faster than sparse dashboards hint—grep Heavy Forwarder logs before rewriting SPL.
grandmaExplanation: We watch which conversations between application groups your tenant agreements allow or refuse, so quiet mistakes—like shortcuts that bypass careful checks or counters that hide real movement—surface before anyone blames the wrong software release.
hardware: Cisco APIC controllers (M3, M4, L4 form factors); Cisco Nexus 9000 ACI-mode switches as leafs (Nexus 9300-FX/FX2/EX/GX/GX2/H series — TCAM capacity ranges from 4096 rules on legacy 9300-FX to 64K+ on 9300-GX2; rule-density limits per-leaf contract enforcement headroom) and spines (Nexus 9332C-aci, 9364C-aci, 9408, 9504/9508/9516 with FM-E2 fabric modules); IPN routers for multi-pod (Nexus 9332C, 9504/9508 acting as IPN). Multi-site ACI uses Cisco Nexus Dashboard Orchestrator (NDO; formerly MSO) for cross-site contract synchronization.
